text:
# Page 1^page=1
Household App Reference Pack Purpose This pack is a staging document for the household app setup. It captures the proposed categories, role lanes, budget structure, list names, and reference documents so the system can be built in pieces without losing the plan. 1) Master Categories Use these categories across tasks, shopping lists, notes, and reference documents so the app stays consistent. Core Household Categories House Admin Budget & Bills Groceries Household Supplies Home Goods & Furniture Move-In / Setup Cleaning / Reset Maintenance / Repair Vehicle / Auto Pet Care Health / Medical Errands Security / Access Tech / IT Scheduling / Calendar Reference / Documents Other Pet Care Subcategories Use Pet Care as the main category, then use the title or description to clarify: Gigi Care Cat Care Food / Supplies Vet / Medical Grooming Schedule / Routine • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • 1

# Page 2^page=2
Budget Categories Start with these: Rent / Housing Utilities Internet / Phone Groceries Household Supplies Pet Care Transportation / Gas Car Insurance Car Maintenance Medical Household Software Furniture / Setup Miscellaneous 2) Role Lanes These are proposed lanes only. Final assignments can be reviewed by the household later. Cody Primary lane: House Admin Budget & Bills oversight App setup and structure Utilities / scheduling / documents Security / access / keys / systems Major setup tracking Mom Primary lane: Groceries Kitchen / food rhythm Household supplies monitoring Daily-use house flow Pet care support Critical contacts input • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • 2

# Page 3^page=3
Zai Primary lane: Vehicle / auto tasks Physical setup items Maintenance support Pet care support Trash / outdoor / practical house tasks Shared / All Shared lane: Weekly reset Cleaning common areas Shopping additions Calendar awareness Furniture / home priorities review Keeping the app updated instead of verbal drive-bys 3) Recommended Lists Shopping Lists Groceries Household Supplies Home Goods & Furniture Vehicle / Auto Needs Pet Care Needs Other / Misc Pickup Notes Use Pet Care Needs instead of a Gigi-only list if you want one place for both animals. Use Other / Misc Pickup only for one-off things that do not fit anywhere else. Task Lists Move-In Punch List Weekly Household Reset Vehicle Maintenance Pet Care Schedule House Admin / Bills / Follow-Up • • • • • • • • • • • 1. 2. 3. 4. 5. 6. • • 1. 2. 3. 4. 5. 3

# Page 4^page=4
Other / Parking Lot Notes Use Pet Care Schedule instead of Gigi-only so both Gigi and the cat fit. Use Other / Parking Lot for things that need a place before they are sorted. 4) Suggested Starter Tasks Move-In Punch List Unpack kitchen essentials — Mom Set up bathroom essentials — Mom Create room-by-room needs list — Cody Install / plan security cameras — Cody Make duplicate house keys — Cody Fill driveway fence gap — Zai Washer / dryer plan — Cody Work chairs / desk setup — Cody Deep clean dust / smoke residue — All Choose furniture priority order — All Smart lock plan — Cody Weekly Household Reset Take trash out — Zai Check fridge and pantry — Mom Restock household supplies — Mom Sweep / vacuum common areas — All Laundry catch-up — All Review upcoming week — Cody Add forgotten tasks to app — All Vehicle Maintenance Record current mileage — Zai Check oil change status — Zai Check tire pressure — Zai Check wiper blades — Zai Confirm insurance and vehicle docs location — Cody Create running service log — Cody Car cleaning / trash reset — Zai Flag renewal dates — Cody 6. • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • 4

# Page 5^page=5
Pet Care Schedule Morning feed — Mom Evening feed — Zai Water bowl check — All Outside / potty breaks — All Food inventory check — Mom Treat check — All Vet info collection — Cody Bath / grooming check — Zai Cat food / litter check — Mom Pet supplies restock review — Cody House Admin / Bills / Follow-Up Add recurring bills and due dates — Cody Confirm grocery rhythm / usual shopping days — Mom Document FamilyWall renewal — Cody Collect critical contacts from Mom — Mom Set household app rules — Cody Review autopays and billing schedule — Cody 5) Budget Setup Reference Budget Purpose The budget should show what is recurring, what is upcoming, and what needs to be reserved monthly so fewer expenses feel like surprises. Recommended Budget Sections Fixed / Recurring Rent / Housing Utilities Internet / Phone Insurance Household Software Variable Groceries Household Supplies Pet Care • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • 5

# Page 6^page=6
Gas / Transportation Car Maintenance Medical Setup / One-Time Furniture Home Setup Cleaning / move-in purchases Security / locks / cameras Sinking Funds Car Maintenance Reserve Pet Care Reserve Household Software Reserve Furniture / Setup Reserve Emergency House Reserve Household Software Example FamilyWall Annual Group Subscription Category: Household Software Amount: 44.99 yearly Monthly reserve: 3.75 Renewal target date: confirm exact date after trial 6) Documents To Create In The App Create these as short reference documents first. 1. Household App Rules Purpose: define what belongs in the app and reduce verbal drive-bys. 2. Role Lanes & Responsibilities Purpose: show proposed lanes for Cody, Mom, Zai, and shared tasks. 3. Recurring Bills & Due Dates Purpose: shared visibility into household financial timing. • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • 6

# Page 7^page=7
4. Budget Categories & Reserves Purpose: define the household budget structure before all numbers are entered. 5. Move-In Priority List Purpose: keep remaining move/setup items visible. 6. Home Goods Priority List Purpose: track what is needed now vs later. 7. Vehicle Info & Maintenance Notes Purpose: track mileage, service needs, and document location. 8. Pet Care Notes Purpose: hold feeding routines, care notes, supply reminders, and vet placeholders. 9. Important Contacts - Pending Review Purpose: hold placeholders until Mom helps complete key contacts. 10. Shopping Categories Reference Purpose: explain how to sort items into groceries, supplies, home goods, auto, pet care, and other. 7) Draft Content For Key Documents Household App Rules Use this app for tasks, reminders, shopping needs, household plans, and shared updates. If it needs to be remembered, bought, done, or scheduled, put it in the app. Check the app before asking a question that may already be answered by the calendar, lists, tasks, or documents. Do not rely on random verbal drive-bys for things that can be entered here. 7

# Page 8^page=8
Role Lanes & Responsibilities This is the proposed starting division of responsibility for household organization. Cody handles house admin, budget oversight, documents, scheduling structure, app setup, security/access, and systems. Mom handles groceries, kitchen rhythm, household supplies monitoring, and helps complete key contacts and household routine details. Zai handles vehicle tasks, practical maintenance support, and physical setup tasks. Shared items remain shared unless reassigned. Budget Categories & Reserves Household budget categories are divided into fixed recurring, variable monthly, one-time setup, and sinking fund reserves. This budget is meant to improve visibility, reduce surprise expenses, and make upcoming costs easier to track. Initial reserve categories include car maintenance, pet care, household software, furniture/setup, and emergency house needs. Pet Care Notes Use Pet Care as the main category for both Gigi and the cat. Pet-related items should be tracked under food/supplies, schedule/routine, grooming, vet/medical, and general care. Feeding, supply restocks, and vet reminders should be visible in the app instead of staying in conversation only. 8) Fast Entry Plan If there is limited time, create these first: 1. Household App Rules 2. Role Lanes & Responsibilities 3. Budget Categories & Reserves 4. Move-In Priority List 5. Pet Care Notes 6. Recurring Bills & Due Dates Then add the task lists and shopping lists later. 8

# Page 9^page=9
9) Final Notes This is a staging pack, not the final perfect system. The goal is to create enough structure that the app feels useful immediately, while leaving room for Mom and Zai to review and adjust details later. 9
